info.description: Git-backed sync server for Formidable — local-first, server-optional.
info.title: GiGot API

post.description: Exchanges username/password for a session cookie.

get.description:
Returns the admin identity associated with the session cookie,
or 401 if no valid session exists. The admin UI polls this on
load to decide whether to show the login form.

GET lists, POST issues, DELETE revokes. Requires a valid
admin session cookie (obtained via /admin/login).

post.description:
GET lists, POST issues, DELETE revokes. Requires a valid
admin session cookie (obtained via /admin/login).

delete.description:
GET lists, POST issues, DELETE revokes. Requires a valid
admin session cookie (obtained via /admin/login).

patch.description:
GET lists, POST issues, DELETE revokes. Requires a valid
admin session cookie (obtained via /admin/login).

post.description: POST issues a new token, DELETE revokes one

delete.description: POST issues a new token, DELETE revokes one

post.description:
Registers a Formidable client so the server can seal responses
to it. Returns the server's public key for outgoing requests.

get.description:
Returns the base64-encoded curve25519 public key that clients
use to seal request bodies.
get.summary: Fetch the server's NaCl public key

post.description: Git smart HTTP protocol — receives packfile data for push operations
Git smart HTTP protocol — serves packfile data for clone and fetch operations

get.description: Git smart HTTP protocol — advertise refs for clone/fetch/push

GET lists all repositories, POST creates a new one. Set
scaffold_formidable: true to seed the fresh repo with a
starter Formidable context (README, templates/basic.yaml,
storage/.gitkeep, and the .formidable/context.json marker)
in an initial commit.

post.description:
GET lists all repositories, POST creates a new one. Set
scaffold_formidable: true to seed the fresh repo with a
starter Formidable context (README, templates/basic.yaml,
storage/.gitkeep, and the .formidable/context.json marker)
in an initial commit.

get.description: GET returns repository details, DELETE removes it

delete.description: GET returns repository details, DELETE removes it

get.description: Returns all branches in a repository

Returns one file's content at the given version (default HEAD),
base64-encoded. 404 covers both missing repo and path not in
version; 422 covers an unresolvable version.

put.description:
Commits one file against the given parent_version. Returns
200 with the new version for a fast-forward or auto-merged
commit (merged_from/merged_with populated on auto-merge).
Returns 409 with base/theirs/yours blobs on a real conflict,
or 409 with only current_version + yours when parent_version
is not an ancestor of HEAD.

get.description:
Returns the current commit SHA and default branch name. Clients
use this as a cheap probe before pulling tree or snapshot. Returns
409 if the repo exists but has no commits yet.
Returns recent commits from a repository

Returns every blob at the given version with content
base64-encoded. Intended for initial client populate and
disaster recovery; prefer /tree + /files/{path} for
incremental syncing.
Returns branches and status of a repository

Returns the recursive blob listing at the given version (or
HEAD when omitted). Clients diff this against their local
snapshot before pulling content. Returns 409 if the repo has
no commits yet, 422 if version does not resolve.

BearerAuth.description: Enter your bearer token as: Bearer <token>
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
